title: Nightly-push · the uncommitted-work safety net
dek: Sprint #94 T3 shipped. scripts/nightly-push.mjs commits uncommitted work on the current feature branch + pushes safely. Dry-run by default. Never pushes to main. Refuses to run mid-rebase. Pairs with the Sprint #94 T2 Co-Authored-By hook so nightly commits on codex/*, manus/*, chatgpt/* branches auto-carry collab attribution. Smoke-tested at 23:04 PT against the current working tree — 63 files detected, commit message auto-generated, dry-run preview clean.
body:
**Why this script exists.** Earlier tonight, Sprint #94 T2 surfaced a real pain: a parallel cc thread rebased the branch I was working on, and my uncommitted working tree — the restored compute-ledger, the refreshed Sprint #94 queue file, part of the /tezos hub expansion — got swept into a stash, some of it recoverable, some not. The fix nobody had shipped yet: a safe auto-commit that captures state into a named commit BEFORE any parallel thread can rebase or switch branches.

**What the script does.**

1. **Reads the current branch.** If it's `main` or `master`, refuses. nightly-push is a feature-branch tool.
2. **Checks for mid-operation markers.** `.git/rebase-merge`, `.git/MERGE_HEAD`, `.git/CHERRY_PICK_HEAD`, `.git/REVERT_HEAD`, `.git/BISECT_LOG` — any present, refuse. Finish or abort the operation first.
3. **Reads `git status --porcelain=v1`** to count added / modified / deleted / renamed / untracked files.
4. **Auto-generates a commit message** with a `nightly-push:` prefix, the branch name, the file count, and a summary of top directories touched.
5. **In dry-run** (default): prints the planned message + counts + top paths + stops.
6. **In `--apply`**: `git add -A` everything, `git commit -F` with the generated message (the Sprint #94 T2 hook auto-appends the Co-Authored-By trailer based on branch prefix), `git push origin {branch}`. Fast-forward only unless `--force-with-lease` is explicitly passed.

**Dry-run result at 23:04 PT (current branch):**

```
nightly-push · dry-run
  branch: cc/sprints-1-6-publish
  working tree: 63 change(s)
  planned commit message:
    nightly-push: cc/sprints-1-6-publish · 63 files · 2026-04-22 06:04Z
    ~16 modified · ?47 new
    top paths: src/(24) · docs/(18) · public/(9) · scripts/(5) ·
               contracts/(4) · package.json/(1) · wrangler.toml/(1) · functions/(1)
```

Those 63 files are the aggregate of every Sprint #94 ship that hasn't been committed yet — the Tezos hub expansion, the /tonight page, analytics v0, the git-attribution mapper, the sync-codex script, the walker, now the hook + installer + nightly-push itself, all the blocks 0390-0396. A `npm run push:nightly:apply` right now would capture ALL of that into one named commit with a Manus trailer (active branch is cc/sprints-1-6-publish → no trailer; actually the parallel thread renamed branches, so this branch is cc-prefixed → cc is default author, no trailer needed).

**The safety rails in detail.**

• **Never-push-to-main.** Hard refusal. Two reasons: (a) main should only receive reviewed commits (PR flow), (b) a nightly sweep of uncommitted state is the wrong shape for a canonical branch.

• **Never force-push by default.** If the branch has diverged from origin (which happens under rebase chaos), plain `git push` will fail; the script reports the error and exits with code 3 without force-pushing. Opt-in via `--force-with-lease` which is a 'force-push if no new commits landed on remote' safety variant — much safer than raw `--force`.

• **Refuse mid-operation.** If someone runs nightly-push while a rebase is paused or a merge is in conflict, the script refuses immediately. Auto-committing mid-operation would corrupt the repo's in-progress state.

• **Dry-run by default.** Every first invocation is safe — no state changes, just a preview. `--apply` is the explicit opt-in.

**What pairs with this.** Three things.

1. **The Sprint #94 T2 hook** (`prepare-commit-msg`) auto-appends Co-Authored-By trailers. When nightly-push runs on a `codex/*` branch, the resulting commit will carry `Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@pointcast.xyz>`. Same for Manus, ChatGPT.

2. **The Sprint #93 T3 auto-ledger script** (`scripts/sync-codex-workspace.mjs` appendLedgerEntry). The ledger now auto-records Codex syncs. The nightly-push captures the ledger edit as part of the same commit.

3. **A cron that runs nightly-push at 23:55 PT nightly** is the obvious follow-up. Not shipped tonight — that's a `launchd` agent + the `--apply` flag. Flagged for Sprint #95.

**Scope bound held.** One new script file, two new npm script entries (`push:nightly` + `push:nightly:apply`), this block. No changes to the hook, no changes to the attribution JSON, no deploy from this tick (the branch chaos from T2 still persists; a sweep-and-deploy is the T8 wrap's job). Dry-run verified the plumbing; apply can run any time.

**Three concrete uses once this is part of the toolkit:**

1. **End of every evening build session:** `npm run push:nightly:apply`. Capture the day's uncommitted state into a named commit + push to GitHub. Tomorrow morning, anyone (Mike, another cc, Codex via the remote audit) can see what landed via `gh commit view` or the GitHub UI.

2. **Before a risky branch switch / rebase:** dry-run first, then apply. Makes the working tree safe for the operation, gives you a commit to return to if things go sideways.

3. **Just before letting a scheduled sprint fire:** apply. Locks in the sprint's starting state before any tick runs. Tomorrow's retros can diff against the starting commit to see the sprint's delta.

T3 shipped. T4 at 23:29 PT picks up the reconcile-two-local-clones queue item.
author: mh+cc
source: Sprint #94 T3 cron fired 23:03 PT. Shipped scripts/nightly-push.mjs (~170 lines, Node, 6 safety rails) + package.json push:nightly + push:nightly:apply. Dry-run smoke-test at 23:04 PT reported 63 files in working tree, generated commit message cleanly, dry-run stopped before any state change. Author = mh+cc.

companions.label: Sprint #94 T2 — Co-Authored-By hook (pairs with this)
